_source.name: NLR ComStock Reference Documentation V.1 + NLR ResStock Technical Reference Guide 2025 + Guidehouse/NFRC Commercial Fenestration Market Study 2021
_source.methodology: Deterministic modal window type by (census_region, vintage_bin), derived from ComStock (commercial) and ResStock (residential) probabilistic distributions. ComStock samples 12 window configurations keyed by climate zone and energy-code vintage; ResStock samples 11 categories keyed by climate zone, vintage, building type, tenure, and income. Both enforce technology adoption timelines: low-E coatings unavailable before ~1980, triple-pane unavailable before ~2004. The modal (most probable) type per (region, vintage) cell is used here as the deterministic default. Cold-climate regions (Northeast, Midwest) get Triple Pane for 2010+ residential to reflect IECC 2012+ prescriptive U-factor requirements that effectively mandate triple-pane or high-perf low-E in CZ5-7.

_notes: Commercial pre-1980: Guidehouse/NFRC study reports ~49% single-pane for pre-1950 stock. ComStock assigns single-pane as modal for pre-1980 buildings across all regions.
Commercial South 1980-1999: ComStock 2025 R3 shows 54.5% double-pane vs 45.5% single-pane for South 1980-1999 commercial stock. Double-pane used as modal to match ComStock ground truth.
Commercial 2010+: ASHRAE 90.1-2010+ and IECC 2012+ prescriptive U-factors effectively require double-pane low-E minimum nationwide. Triple-pane remains <1% of commercial stock per Guidehouse.

Residential pre-1980: ResStock 2025 R1 shows 56-63% double-pane for pre-1980 NE/MW/West (window retrofits in existing stock). South pre-1980 remains single-pane modal. Double-pane used for NE/MW/West to match ResStock ground truth.
Residential 2010+: ResStock 2025 R1 shows 95-97% double-pane across all regions including cold climates. Triple-pane is <5% even in CZ5-7. Double-pane used nationwide to match ResStock ground truth.
Vintage bins match the RECS/CBECS categorization used elsewhere in the resolver: pre-1980, 1980-1999, 2000-2009, 2010+.
